subtasks.description: Evaluate contractor's download request against access control policies: verify temporary access scope, data classification restrictions, and backup export permissions. Determine if request violates role-based access control (RBAC) or data governance policies.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask (mid-tier) is sufficient. This is explicitly a governance/access control evaluation—the contractor is requesting a download that may violate policy. Mid-tier reasoning is needed to reconcile temporary access scope against data classification policies and role-based restrictions. The request is clear enough that no prior data retrieval is needed; the policy engine can evaluate the action directly against the access control registry."

output.reason: Contractor 'contractor-dave' attempting to download production database backup violates multiple access control policies: (1) POL-701 restricts backups to full-time DBA employees only; contractor role is not eligible; (2) POL-702 explicitly limits contractors to de-identified query results and prohibits raw data exports; full backup is raw production data; (3) POL-703 requires explicit project manager approval and 7-day advance notice; no evidence of approval or notice provided.
output.escalation_rationale: If legitimate business need exists, redirect contractor through formal approval workflow: obtain written project manager approval, provide 7-day notice, and consider alternative: provide de-identified data subset via secure query interface instead of full backup export.
